voiceandtone.error: When operational input fails, there is a risk of data loss, or systemic issues arise.
voiceandtone.everyday: When users are handling daily tasks.
voiceandtone.guide: Guidelines to support WIM UI's 'tangible UI' from a communication perspective.
voiceandtone.guide_desc: Defines consistent personality (Voice) and contextual tone (Tone) so users can trust the system and operate with confidence.
voiceandtone.new: When users employ new features, or complex operations are demanded.
voiceandtone.personality: At the core of WIM UI is the personality of a 'Reliable partner who assists user tasks and guides to sure success'.
voiceandtone.point1: Unobtrusively and flatly conveys only the minimum required info to let the users proceed at their own pace.
voiceandtone.point2: Briefly communicates a sense of security 'It is indeed completed' without extra hype, while sharing a feel of achievement.
voiceandtone.point3: Strictly avoid wording that blames users. Clearly present the fact of what happened and actionable specifics on how to solve it.
voiceandtone.point4: Guides users so they can confidently operate, by breaking down steps minutely and carefully explaining jargon.
voiceandtone.success: When a task is completed, or operational input is properly received.
voiceandtone.tone_desc: While maintaining the consistent Voice personality, the tone alters matching the user's situation and feelings.
voiceandtone.voice_clear_desc: Avoid jargon and roundabout expressions; choose words anyone can understand.
voiceandtone.voice_empathetic_desc: Present solutions from the user perspective instead of imposing system convenience during errors.
voiceandtone.voice_helpful_desc: Organize info so users can tell what to do next at a glance.
voiceandtone.voice_honest_desc: Avoid exaggeration or overly decorated words; convey facts accurately.
voiceandtone.voice_reliable_desc: Foster trust by maintaining consistent wording.
voiceandtone.voice_supportive_desc: Maintain an attitude of always supporting user goals.
